# Ai Source Index

Converted from `28_ai_source_index.csv` so the pack remains Markdown-only.

| key | category | priority | title | authors | year | type | access | url | bias | why |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| grewal_satornino_davenport_guha_2025 | Generative AI in marketing | Essential | How generative AI Is shaping the future of marketing | Dhruv Grewal; Cinthia B. Satornino; Thomas Davenport; Abhijit Guha | 2025 | Peer-reviewed conceptual/review article | Springer page; open/full text may vary | https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s11747-024-01064-3 | Academic; broad strategy lens; not a tactical implementation manual | High-level map of how GenAI changes marketing research, strategy, actions, customer experience, and firm processes. |
| cillo_rubera_2025 | Generative AI in marketing | High | Generative AI in innovation and marketing processes | Paola Cillo; Gaia Rubera | 2025 | Peer-reviewed roadmap/research opportunities | Springer page | https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s11747-024-01044-7 | Academic; opportunity framing | Useful for seeing GenAI as part of product, innovation, and marketing processes rather than copy generation alone. |
| yang_2025_transforming_advertising_gai | Generative AI in advertising practice | High | Transforming advertising in the age of generative AI | J. Yang | 2025 | Peer-reviewed exploratory study | Taylor & Francis page | https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/02650487.2025.2604910 | Qualitative/exploratory; not causal performance evidence | Shows how advertising practitioners integrate GenAI into creative and production workflows. |
| reisenbichler_reutterer_schweidel_dan_2022 | AI content marketing | Essential | Frontiers: Supporting Content Marketing with Natural Language Generation | Martin Reisenbichler; Thomas Reutterer; David A. Schweidel; Daniel Dan | 2022 | Peer-reviewed Marketing Science article | Open author PDF | https://www.reutterer.com/papers/reisenbichler%26reutterer%26schweidel%26dan_2022.pdf | Academic; pre-ChatGPT NLG context but still methodologically useful | Grounds AI content use in semi-automated content marketing and SEO rather than vague productivity claims. |
| brand_israel_ngwe_2023_llm_market_research | AI market research | Essential | Using LLMs for Market Research | James Brand; Ayelet Israeli; Donald Ngwe | 2023 | Harvard Business School working paper | Open PDF | https://www.hbs.edu/ris/Publication%20Files/23-062_6bfe7a5b-3ed9-4afd-a4c1-c91b60dd82e5.pdf | Academic working paper; methods still developing | Shows how LLMs may be used to simulate or augment market research, while requiring careful validation against real human data. |
| wang_2024_llm_market_research_arxiv | AI market research | High | Large Language Models for Market Research: A Data Augmentation Approach | M. Wang et al. | 2024 | arXiv research paper | Open arXiv | https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.19363 | Preprint; not final peer-reviewed; technical-statistical focus | Important warning: LLM-generated responses can help, but must be debiased/anchored with real human data. |
| aggarwal_2023_geo | AI search / GEO | Essential | GEO: Generative Engine Optimization | P. Aggarwal et al. | 2023/2024 | arXiv / research paper | Open PDF | https://arxiv.org/pdf/2311.09735 | Early research; engines and products evolve quickly | Introduces the idea that visibility in generative answer engines requires different tactics from classic SEO. |
| chen_wang_chen_koudas_2025_geo_ai_search | AI search / GEO | Essential | Generative Engine Optimization: How to Dominate AI Search | Mahe Chen; Xiaoxuan Wang; Kaiwen Chen; Nick Koudas | 2025 | arXiv empirical analysis | Open arXiv | https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.08919 | Preprint; commercial relevance high but findings may shift as engines update | Compares AI search and traditional search; emphasizes earned media, source authority, freshness, and machine-readable justification. |
| nist_genai_profile_2024 | AI governance and risk | Essential | Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework: Generative Artificial Intelligence Profile | NIST | 2024 | Government risk management framework | Open PDF | https://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/ai/NIST.AI.600-1.pdf | Government/standards perspective; not marketing-specific | Core risk-management source for GenAI use: validity, privacy, security, transparency, and governance. |
| nist_ai_rmf | AI governance and risk | Essential | NIST AI Risk Management Framework | NIST | 2023/2024 | Government standards framework | Open web page and PDFs | https://www.nist.gov/itl/ai-risk-management-framework | Government/standards perspective | Use as your baseline for trustworthy AI management across design, deployment, monitoring, and governance. |
| owasp_llm_top_10_2025 | AI security | Essential | OWASP Top 10 for Large Language Model Applications | OWASP GenAI Security Project | 2025 | Security guidance | Open web page and PDF | https://owasp.org/www-project-top-10-for-large-language-model-applications/ | Security-focused; conservative by design | Needed if building AI chatbots, sales agents, support agents, website agents, or internal marketing automations. |
| stanford_ai_index_2025 | AI market context | Essential | The 2025 AI Index Report | Stanford HAI | 2025 | Annual AI index report | Open web page/PDF | https://hai.stanford.edu/ai-index/2025-ai-index-report | Academic/institutional; broad AI scope, not marketing-specific | Current macro baseline for AI adoption, investment, capabilities, policy, and public perception. |
| mckinsey_state_ai_2025 | AI adoption / business | High | The State of AI: Global Survey 2025 | McKinsey & Company / QuantumBlack | 2025 | Consultancy survey/report | Open article/PDF | https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/quantumblack/our-insights/the-state-of-ai | Consultancy; useful signal but incentive to frame AI transformation opportunities | Current business adoption signal; useful for seeing which functions use AI and how firms report value. |
| mit_nanda_genai_divide_2025 | AI adoption / business | High | The GenAI Divide: State of AI in Business 2025 | MIT Project NANDA | 2025 | Research report | Open PDF mirror | https://mlq.ai/media/quarterly_decks/v0.1_State_of_AI_in_Business_2025_Report.pdf | Preliminary findings; methodology should be read carefully | Counterweight to hype: argues many GenAI projects fail because they are not integrated into workflows. |
| ftc_operation_ai_comply_2024 | AI advertising law and ethics | Essential | FTC Announces Crackdown on Deceptive AI Claims and Schemes | Federal Trade Commission | 2024 | Regulator enforcement release | Open web page | https://www.ftc.gov/news-events/news/press-releases/2024/09/ftc-announces-crackdown-deceptive-ai-claims-schemes | Regulator perspective; enforcement-focused | Essential warning against overclaiming AI capabilities, earnings outcomes, fake reviews, and AI-washing. |
| ftc_ai_industry_page | AI advertising law and ethics | High | FTC Artificial Intelligence Industry Page | Federal Trade Commission | Current | Regulator hub | Open web page | https://www.ftc.gov/industry/technology/artificial-intelligence | Regulator perspective | Use to monitor AI enforcement trends around deception, data use, endorsements, and consumer harm. |
| ftc_truth_advertising | Advertising law and ethics | Essential | Truth in Advertising | Federal Trade Commission | Current | Regulator guidance hub | Open web page | https://www.ftc.gov/news-events/topics/truth-advertising | Regulator perspective | Base rule: advertising claims must be truthful, not misleading, and substantiated where appropriate. |
| oecd_ai_principles | AI governance and risk | High | OECD AI Principles | OECD | 2019/2024 update | International policy principles | Open web page | https://oecd.ai/en/ai-principles | Policy lens; high-level | Good global baseline for trustworthy AI: human rights, transparency, robustness, accountability. |
| eu_ai_act_framework | AI regulation | High | EU AI Act regulatory framework | European Commission | 2024-2026 | Regulatory framework page | Open web page | https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/regulatory-framework-ai | EU regulatory perspective | Required if marketing or AI products reach EU users; useful baseline for risk-based AI governance. |
| eu_gpai_code_practice | AI regulation | Useful/current | General-Purpose AI Code of Practice | European Commission | 2025 | Regulatory code of practice | Open web page and downloadable chapters | https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/contents-code-gpai | EU regulatory perspective; provider-focused | Useful for understanding transparency, copyright, and safety expectations around general-purpose AI models. |
| google_ads_ai_overviews | AI search / ads | High | About ads and AI Overviews | Google Ads Help | Current | Platform documentation | Open web page | https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/16297775?hl=en | Platform-owned; describes Google’s product framing | Source for how ads are integrated into AI Overviews and how AI search changes ad surfaces. |
| google_marketing_live_2025 | AI search / ads | Useful/current | Google Marketing Live 2025: News and Updates | Google | 2025 | Platform announcement | Open web page | https://blog.google/products/ads-commerce/google-marketing-live-2025/ | Platform marketing announcement; product-promotion bias | Tracks current AI ad products, AI Mode/AI Overviews advertising, and campaign automation direction. |
| openai_instant_checkout_2025 | Agentic commerce | High | Buy it in ChatGPT: Instant Checkout and the Agentic Commerce Protocol | OpenAI | 2025 | Platform announcement | Open web page | https://openai.com/index/buy-it-in-chatgpt/ | Platform-owned; product-promotion bias | Signals the movement from AI-assisted discovery to AI-mediated checkout and agentic commerce. |
| openai_product_discovery_2026 | Agentic commerce | High/current | Powering Product Discovery in ChatGPT | OpenAI | 2026 | Platform announcement | Open web page | https://openai.com/index/powering-product-discovery-in-chatgpt/ | Platform-owned; product-promotion bias | Current signal for AI-native product discovery, comparison, and shopping behavior. |
| mckinsey_agentic_commerce_2025 | Agentic commerce | High | The agentic commerce opportunity: How AI agents are ushering in a new era for consumers and merchants | McKinsey & Company | 2025 | Consultancy report | Open web page | https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/quantumblack/our-insights/the-agentic-commerce-opportunity-how-ai-agents-are-ushering-in-a-new-era-for-consumers-and-merchants | Consultancy; opportunity framing | Useful for thinking beyond ads: buyer agents, merchant data, checkout, product feeds, and trust. |
| g2_buyer_behavior_2025 | B2B software buying / AI | High/current | 2025 G2 Buyer Behavior Report | G2 | 2025 | Vendor/review-platform survey report | Open PDF | https://images.g2crowd.com/uploads/attachment/file/1470753/2025-G2-Buyer-Behavior-Report.pdf | Review platform; commercially biased toward review/research channels | Useful current signal that AI search and review sites increasingly shape B2B software evaluation. |
| trustradius_b2b_ai_2025 | B2B software buying / AI | High/current | B2B Tech Buying in the Age of AI | TrustRadius | 2025 | Vendor/review-platform buyer report | Open PDF | https://go.trustradius.com/rs/827-FOI-687/images/TrustRadius-2025-Buyer-Research-Report-Top-10-Takeaways.pdf | Review platform; commercially biased, but useful buyer-behavior signal | Highlights AI Overviews, fact-checking, prior experience, pricing transparency, and the trust gap in B2B tech buying. |
| gartner_b2b_rep_free_2026 | B2B software buying / AI | High/current | Gartner Sales Survey Finds 67% of B2B Buyers Prefer a Rep-Free Experience | Gartner | 2026 | Analyst press release | Open web page | https://www.gartner.com/en/newsroom/press-releases/2026-03-09-gartner-sales-survey-finds-67-percent-of-b2b-buyers-prefer-a-rep-free-experience | Analyst firm; press release is summarized, not full methodology | Reinforces self-serve/software-buyer trend and the need to build AI-assisted buyer support instead of relying only on reps. |
| iab_genai_ad_playbook_2025 | Generative AI advertising practice | Useful/current | Generative AI Playbook for Advertising | IAB | 2025 | Industry playbook | Open PDF | https://www.iab.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/01/IAB_GenerativeAIPlaybook_January_26.pdf | Industry association; practice-heavy, less neutral than academic sources | Operational view of GenAI use cases, campaign optimization, production, creative, and governance. |
| doshi_hauser_2024_creativity | AI creativity | High | Generative AI enhances individual creativity but reduces the collective diversity of novel content | Anil R. Doshi; Oliver P. Hauser | 2024 | Peer-reviewed Science Advances article | Publisher page | https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/sciadv.adn5290 | Specific creative-writing context; generalize carefully | Important caution: AI can improve average output but flatten diversity. This matters for ads and brand differentiation. |
| kirk_givi_2025_ai_authorship | AI disclosure / trust | High | The AI-authorship effect: Understanding authenticity, moral disgust, and consumer responses to AI-generated marketing communications | C. P. Kirk; J. Givi | 2025 | Peer-reviewed Journal of Business Research article | Abstract/index page | https://ideas.repec.org/a/eee/jbrese/v186y2025ics0148296324004880.html | Experimental context; emotional communications may not generalize to all software ads | Shows that AI authorship can reduce authenticity, positive word of mouth, and loyalty for some emotional communications. |
| schilke_2025_transparency_dilemma | AI disclosure / trust | High | The transparency dilemma: How AI disclosure erodes trust | Oliver Schilke et al. | 2025 | Peer-reviewed article | ScienceDirect page | https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0749597825000172 | Context dependent; transparency can be legally/ethically necessary even if trust penalty exists | Nuanced source: disclosure can reduce trust, so transparency strategy must be handled carefully, not mechanically. |
| baek_2024_ai_disclosure_prosocial_ads | AI disclosure / trust | Useful | Effect of disclosing AI-generated content on prosocial advertising | T. H. Baek et al. | 2024 | Peer-reviewed advertising study | Taylor & Francis page | https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/02650487.2024.2401319 | Prosocial advertising context; not directly SaaS | Good example of how disclosure effects are context-sensitive and can influence attitudes/intentions. |
| chen_2024_ai_ad_appeals | AI advertising effectiveness | Useful | Appeal types, self-efficacy and AI's social role | Y. Chen et al. | 2024 | Peer-reviewed experimental article | ScienceDirect page | https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0148296324003710 | Experimental; limited contexts | Useful for choosing whether AI-created ads should use practical/agentic vs warm/communal appeals. |
| madathil_2025_human_ai_ads | AI advertising effectiveness | Useful | Generative AI advertisements and Human–AI collaboration | J. C. Madathil et al. | 2025 | Peer-reviewed advertising study | ScienceDirect page | https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0969698925001602 | Specific to video ads and human-AI cues | Useful for thinking about when human involvement should remain visible to protect perceived humanity and reduce anxiety. |
| exner_ai_in_disguise_2025 | AI advertising effectiveness | High | How AI-generated ads' visual cues shape consumer behavior | Y. Exner et al. | 2025 | Research paper / quasi-experimental advertising data | Open PDF | https://www.columbia.edu/~on2110/Papers/AI_in_disguise.pdf | Specific dataset and ad context; quasi-experimental assumptions matter | Useful because it links AI-generated visual cues to real ad behavior rather than opinions alone. |
| collaborating_ai_agents_ads_2025 | Human-AI collaboration | High | Collaborating with AI Agents: A Field Experiment on Ad Creation | Research authors via arXiv | 2025 | arXiv/field experiment | Open arXiv HTML | https://arxiv.org/html/2503.18238v2 | Preprint; evaluate methods carefully | Useful field evidence on human-AI collaboration in ad creation, productivity, and output quality. |
| fang_2025_genai_firm_productivity | AI productivity / workflows | High | Generative AI and Firm Productivity: Field Experiments in Online Retail | L. Fang et al. | 2025 | arXiv/field experiment | Open arXiv HTML / PDF | https://arxiv.org/html/2510.12049v1 | Preprint; online-retail setting; productivity != brand trust | Shows GenAI value emerges from embedded workflows, not isolated prompts. |
| adobe_b2b_ai_digital_trends_2025 | B2B AI marketing practice | Useful/current | 2025 AI and Digital Trends in B2B Journeys | Adobe | 2025 | Vendor report | Report page/gated in places | https://business.adobe.com/resources/reports/b2b-marketing-digital-trends.html | Vendor; promotes enterprise marketing software worldview | Useful market signal for AI-driven B2B journeys, content effectiveness, and personalization. |
| consumer_chatbot_acceptance_2025 | AI chatbots / conversational commerce | Useful | Consumer Acceptance of Conversational Bots: Systematic Literature Review | O. H. Fares et al. | 2025/2026 | Systematic literature review | Publisher page | https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1002/cb.70130 | Review article; customer-service/chatbot context varies | Useful for designing AI chat interfaces that buyers can trust and use rather than resent. |
